content: This document contains information about the application architecture
The Interface Layer (e.g., API handlers) receive requests and pass them to the Usecase Layer.
The Usecase Layer contains application-specific business logic. It coordinates operations using domain entities and interfaces.
The Domain Layer defines core business entities (e.g., Tenant, User) and interfaces. It is independent of other layers.

The Repository Layer provides concrete implementations for data storage (e.g., Postgres, Redis), fulfilling interfaces defined in the domain layer. The usecase layer interacts with repositories only through these interfaces.
This separation ensures that business logic is isolated from infrastructure concerns, making the codebase easier to test and maintain.
`mtsg` is built using Clean Architecture principles, dividing the application into well-separated layers:
internal/ domain/ # Core entities and interfaces (Tenant, User, etc.) usecase/ # Application logic (Login, CreateProject) repository/ # Postgres + Redis implementations
